title: sida rhombifolia var. pohliana
intro: Sida rhombifolia var. pohliana, also known as Sida acuta, is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Sida rhombifolia var. pohliana include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and infusions of its leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves for various health ailments.
